title: ophrys subfusca subsp. lucentina
intro: Ophrys subfusca subsp. lucentina is a subspecies of orchid that belongs to the genus Ophrys, commonly known as the 'bee orchid' due to its unique flower structure resembling a female bee. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, with its roots and leaves containing compounds with potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Ophrys subfusca subsp. lucentina may include reducing inflammation, treating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, teas, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
